children.name: Steatornis
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the remarkable oilbird, the only nocturnal, fruit-eating bird that navigates by echolocation. It is one of the most unusual birds in the world, with evolutionary affinities still debated.

children.name: Steatornis caripensis
children.description: A peculiar, chestnut-brown bird with a hooked bill and long, stiff tail feathers, found in caves and gorges across the Andes from Venezuela to Bolivia. It is the only nocturnal fruit-eating bird and navigates in total darkness using audible clicking sounds akin to bat echolocation. Chicks were historically rendered for their high-quality oil, giving the species its common name.
